name: AZQ Stage 2 Wave C Checks
purpose: Define task-specific automated checks for Stage 2 Wave C so the Codex runner can evaluate Formam closeout work, including regression coverage, documentation alignment, and runner resilience, without hardcoding task logic into the runner.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the patch.

STAGE2-WAVEC-01.description: Add Stage 2 regression test suite for canonical Formam storage and CLI flows.

checks.name: stage2_test_file_exists
checks.description: Verify the Stage 2 regression test file exists.

checks.name: stage2_regression_suite
checks.description: Verify the Stage 2 regression suite exists and passes.

checks.name: stage2_test_file_covers_formam_flows
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('tests/test_stage2_wave_c.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
for snippet in [
    'normalize_deliverable_record',
    'parse_deliverable_record',
    'next_deliverable_id',
    'validate_parent_goal',
    'parse_goal_map_record',
    'form build',
    'form list',
    'form show',
    'form map'
]:
    assert snippet in text, snippet
PY
checks.description: Verify the regression suite covers the main Stage 2 storage and CLI paths called out by the closeout task.

human_checks: The tests stay focused on canonical Formam storage and CLI-visible behavior rather than speculative planning features.
The new regression suite is readable enough that later Agenda work can extend it without rewriting the whole file.

STAGE2-WAVEC-02.description: Align Stage 2 documentation with canonical Formam storage.

checks.name: readme_teaches_live_formam_storage
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('README.md').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
for snippet in [
    'data/form/deliverables/',
    'data/form/maps/',
    'azq form build',
    'azq form list',
    'azq form show',
    'azq form map'
]:
    assert snippet in text, snippet
PY
checks.description: Verify README.md teaches the live canonical Formam storage model and command set.

checks.name: bootstrap_teaches_live_formam_artifacts
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('AZQ_BOOTSTRAP.md').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
for snippet in [
    'data/form/deliverables/',
    'data/form/maps/'
]:
    assert snippet in text, snippet
PY
checks.description: Verify AZQ_BOOTSTRAP.md names the canonical Formam artifact directories.

checks.name: stage2_build_tasks_no_longer_describes_pre_formam_gaps
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('AZQ_BUILD_TASKS_STAGE_2.md').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
for stale in [
    'there is no `azq/formam/` package in the live repository',
    'no `data/form/deliverables/` directory exists',
    'no `data/form/maps/` directory exists',
    'the CLI does not expose any `azq form ...` commands yet'
]:
    assert stale not in text, stale
PY
checks.description: Verify AZQ_BUILD_TASKS_STAGE_2.md no longer describes the pre-implementation Formam gaps after Stage 2 closeout.

human_checks: The docs preserve the craft order clearly by teaching deliverables and maps as structure artifacts, not execution tasks.
The rewritten language still sounds like the rest of the AZQ docs rather than like generated release notes.

STAGE2-WAVEC-03.description: Add graceful Stage 2 closeout behavior to the ritual runner.

checks.name: runner_handles_exhausted_stage2_wave_cleanly
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory
repo = Path.cwd()
with TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    workspace = Path(tmp)
    tasks = [
        {'task_id': 'STAGE2-WAVEC-01', 'title': 'Task 1'},
        {'task_id': 'STAGE2-WAVEC-02', 'title': 'Task 2'},
        {'task_id': 'STAGE2-WAVEC-03', 'title': 'Task 3'}
    ]
    state = {
        'tasks': {
            'STAGE2-WAVEC-01': {'status': 'passed_checks', 'updated_at': '2026-03-17T00:00:00'},
            'STAGE2-WAVEC-02': {'status': 'passed_checks', 'updated_at': '2026-03-17T00:00:00'},
            'STAGE2-WAVEC-03': {'status': 'passed_checks', 'updated_at': '2026-03-17T00:00:00'}
        },
        'history': []
    }
    (workspace / 'AZQ_BUILD_TASKS_STAGE_2_WAVE_C.json').write_text(json.dumps(tasks), encoding='utf-8')
    (workspace / '.azq_codex_progress_stage2_wave_c.json').write_text(json.dumps(state), encoding='utf-8')
    proc = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, str(repo / 'azq_codex_stage1_task_runner.py'), 'run', '--stage', '2', '--wave', 'wave_c', '--workspace', str(workspace)],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        cwd=str(repo)
    )
    assert proc.returncode == 0, proc.stdout + proc.stderr
    assert 'No remaining' in proc.stdout, proc.stdout
    assert 'Stage 2 Complete' in proc.stdout, proc.stdout
    assert (workspace / 'codex_reports' / 'codex_azq_task_status_report.md').is_file()
PY
checks.description: Verify the Python runner treats an exhausted Stage 2 Wave C backlog as successful closeout and still writes a report.

checks.name: ritual_script_is_stage_aware
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('run_next_task_in_current_wave.sh').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
for snippet in [
    'discover_stage_waves',
    '--stage "$CURRENT_STAGE"',
    'No remaining staged tasks.'
]:
    assert snippet in text, snippet
PY
checks.description: Verify the ritual shell path remains stage-aware and does not hardcode a Stage 1-only completion flow.

human_checks: The closeout output remains concise and operator-friendly in the terminal.
The runner changes stay narrow and do not redesign the staged-work ritual beyond the exhausted-wave success path.
